name: Lac de la Forêt — loop — running from Aignan
name_original: Lac de la Forêt — boucle — course à pied depuis Aignan
region_intro: Jogging routes around Aignan traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills and agricultural land, often featuring paths that wind through forests and past local lakes. The region's terrain offers a mix of gentle gradients and more challenging ascents, providing varied running experiences. Many routes are circular, allowing runners to explore the area's natural beauty and quiet countryside. Best jogging routes around Aignan The most popular jogging route is Lac de la Forêt — loop — running from Aignan, a 5.0 miles (8.0 km) trail that takes about 56 minutes to complete. This route offers a scenic loop around Lac de la Forêt. Another top favourite among local runners is Running loop from Sabazan, a difficult 11.5 miles (18.5 km) path. This route presents a challenging run through varied terrain with significant elevation changes. Local runners also love the Lac de la Forêt loop from Lac de la Forêt, a 3.3 miles (5.3 km) trail leading through forested areas around the lake, often completed in about 38 minutes. Jogging around Aignan is defined by rolling hills, tranquil lakes, and quiet country roads. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy jogs to more difficult running challenges. The routes in Aignan are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 180 runners have used komoot to explore Aignan's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Aignan?
A: There are over 50 running routes around Aignan, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels. These include 4 easy routes, 47 moderate routes, and 4 difficult routes, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners in Aignan?
A: Yes, Aignan offers 4 easy running routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed jog. These trails typically feature gentler gradients and shorter distances, allowing for an enjoyable introduction to the region's scenic countryside.

Q: What are some good options for a 5km running route in Aignan?
A: For a run around 5km, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20165579" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac de la Forêt loop from Lac de la Forêt&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate 5.3 km (3.3 miles) trail takes about 38 minutes to complete and leads through forested areas around the lake, offering a pleasant and scenic experience.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Aignan?
A: Many of the running routes around Aignan are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197161767" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac de la Forêt — loop — running from Aignan&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 8 km (5 miles) circular trail that offers a scenic loop around the lake.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails around Aignan?
A: The running trails around Aignan feature a diverse landscape of rolling hills and agricultural land. You'll often find paths winding through forests and past local lakes. The terrain offers a mix of gentle gradients and more challenging ascents, providing varied running experiences.

Q: What are some interesting places to see along the running routes in Aignan?
A: While jogging, you can explore several points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5860359" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac de la Forêt&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent feature on several routes. Other nearby attractions include the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5456217" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Termes d'Armagnac&lt;/a&gt; castle, the charming settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3553918" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lupiac&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6047743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Plaisance-du-Gers&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any running routes with good viewpoints in the Aignan area?
A: Yes, the region offers opportunities for scenic views. You might encounter a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5860372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;panoramic view of the Pyrenees and surrounding countryside&lt;/a&gt; on some of the higher points along the trails, especially on routes that traverse the rolling hills.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Aignan?
A: The running routes in Aignan are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the tranquil lakes, quiet country roads, and the variety of terrain that caters to different ability levels, from easy jogs to more difficult challenges.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: For experienced runners seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20165776" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Sabazan&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 18.5 km (11.5 miles) path. This route presents a demanding run through varied terrain with significant elevation changes, perfect for pushing your limits.

Q: Can you recommend a moderate-difficulty running route in Aignan?
A: A good moderate-difficulty option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/36074025" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Meridian marker loop from Loubédat&lt;/a&gt;. This 11.5 km (7.1 miles) route takes approximately 1 hour and 13 minutes to complete, offering a balanced challenge with varied terrain and elevation.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Aignan?
A: Aignan's climate makes spring and autumn particularly pleasant for running, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer mornings and evenings are also ideal to avoid the midday heat, while winter can offer crisp, quiet runs, though some paths might be muddy after rain.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by a lake in Aignan?
A: Yes, several running routes feature the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5860359" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac de la Forêt&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197161767" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac de la Forêt — loop — running from Aignan&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20165579" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac de la Forêt loop from Lac de la Forêt&lt;/a&gt; both offer scenic paths around the lake, providing tranquil views during your run.
tips: When you reach this point, the view opens up and, in good weather, you can see the Pyrenees in the distance.
Beautiful lake surrounded by trees on its shores which are reflected in the water. A magical place to run.

waypoints.name: Panoramic view of the Pyrenees and surrounding countryside
